title: Hedera cochleata
taxonomy: Hedera cochleata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. It is a member of the genus Hedera and is closely related to other English ivy species. Hedera cochleata is native to East Asia and has been naturalized in various parts of the world. The plant is a climber with evergreen leaves and is often used as an ornamental in gardens and landscapes. It is also known for its ability to form dense mats and can be invasive in some areas.
uses: Hedera cochleata, also known as English ivy, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in compounds with potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
